title: Reading Commercial Leases
shortSummary: Learn how to read leases for action: recoverables, rent reviews, options, make-good, approvals, and landlord obligations.
whyItMatters: The lease is the operating contract for the asset. If you miss a critical date, misread an obligation, or fail to interpret a clause correctly, the cost is real: disputes, missed recoveries, service failures, and avoidable vacancy risk. Strong property managers translate lease language into calendars, checklists, and conversations.
coreConcepts: Key commercial lease clauses every property manager should track
Critical dates: rent reviews, expiry, option notice windows, and bank guarantees
How to summarise a lease so operations can use it
